mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is designed for scenarios where performance and resource efficiency are critical, making it suitable for production-grade applications. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware optimizations and potential complexity in setup.

steps.title: Add Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install vLLM using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.19.1

```

> This command adds the specific version of the vLLM repository.

steps.title: Install vLLM CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for vLLM to function properly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.19.1/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs necessary for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the vLLM application in a specified namespace, setting it up for use in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.19.1

```

> This installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for vLLM, which defines the model and its configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  parameters:
    max_length: 512
EOF

```

> This creates a sample vLLM resource with a specified model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  parameters:
    max_length: 512
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM Custom Resource
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the vLLM Custom Resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> This lists the vLLM resources in the 'vllm' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM installation from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources, which may result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current configurations. This process involves draining the nodes, upgrading the Helm release, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.19.1

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Replace <node-name> with your actual node name to cordon and drain it.
> This command upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Replace <node-name> with your actual node name to uncordon it after the upgrade.
> This verifies that the vLLM resource is running correctly after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm.io/v1: no matches for kind 'VLLM' in version 'vllm.io/v1'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the CRDs for vLLM have not been installed or are not available.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.19.1/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <IP>:<port>: i/o timeout'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This suggests that the Kubernetes API server is unreachable, possibly due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your network connectivity and ensure that your kubeconfig is correctly set up.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the vLLM release has not been installed in the specified namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure that you have installed vLLM using Helm and that you are querying the correct namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create VLLM: insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This means that the Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the vLLM pods.

**Fix:**
Check your cluster's resource allocation and consider scaling up your nodes.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new quantization methods and improved model serving performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required when upgrading from earlier versions.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME                READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE' with your vLLM resource listed as '1/1' under the READY column.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  parameters:
    max_length: 512
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required tools installed.
